amendments.body: Forward-compatible journal repair is still not implemented. `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` remain `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:377` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:412`, so an older binary cannot parse journal details written by a newer binary and `append_event_if_missing` falls through to appending a duplicate event instead of repairing the existing row. The merge path in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2338-2407` also still relies on the generic `json_fill_none(...)` helper, so any future field with conflict semantics can be silently mishandled unless every caller is manually updated. Amendment 1 required forward-compatible deserialization plus explicit field-by-field merge rules; that gap remains.
amendments.rationale: This still breaks rollback/upgrade interoperability for milestone journals and leaves the repair path brittle for future schema additions.

amendments.body: Terminal replays can still synthesize a `plan_hash` on already-finalized rows. `TaskRunEntry::merge_attempt_entries()` now copies `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` onto the merged row (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:328-330`), and `update_task_run()` still calls `safe_plan_hash_backfill()` inside the already-finalized replay branch (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3371-3379`). Because `safe_plan_hash_backfill()` fills any row whose `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` matches the current snapshot (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2636-2642`), replaying a terminal completion with `plan_hash=None` can mutate a historical terminal row from `None` to the snapshot hash. The accepted contract for Amendment 3 was the opposite: terminal replays must preserve the caller-supplied `None`, and only open-row repairs may auto-populate `plan_hash`.
amendments.rationale: Replay/repair would silently rewrite lineage for completed attempts and can misstate which plan version actually finished the bead.

payload.summary: Working-tree `git diff` was empty, so I reviewed the latest implementation delta (`2af09d5..f81a247`) and the current milestone lineage/journal code. Two correctness gaps remain: journal detail parsing is still not forward-compatible, and terminal replay still auto-populates `plan_hash` on finalized rows.
